raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"'Stronger' plausibly demands a sharper thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim — the 'first to…' framing and deployment-gap positioning are the core argumentative load this text must carry.","R1.2":"Logical tightening (premises → conclusions, e.g. the jump from training commitment to deployment gap) is a natural reading of 'stronger'; gaps in the inferential chain weaken rhetorical force.","R1.4":"The artifact makes explicit novelty claims ('to our knowledge, the first…'); strengthening those claims requires verifying and articulating the delta vs. prior work more precisely.","R1.6":"Sentence- and paragraph-level prose improvement is an equally natural reading of 'stronger'; vague constructions ('differs substantially', 'only modest') and passive hedging could be the intended target."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus primarily on sharpening the contribution and novelty claims — tightening the logical argument and 'first-to' positioning (content strength) — or on improving sentence-level clarity, word choice, and rhetorical impact (writing strength)?"}

rationale.R1.1: 'Stronger' plausibly demands a sharper thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim — the 'first to…' framing and deployment-gap positioning are the core argumentative load this text must carry.
rationale.R1.2: Logical tightening (premises → conclusions, e.g. the jump from training commitment to deployment gap) is a natural reading of 'stronger'; gaps in the inferential chain weaken rhetorical force.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes explicit novelty claims ('to our knowledge, the first…'); strengthening those claims requires verifying and articulating the delta vs. prior work more precisely.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level prose improvement is an equally natural reading of 'stronger'; vague constructions ('differs substantially', 'only modest') and passive hedging could be the intended target.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on sharpening the contribution and novelty claims — tightening the logical argument and 'first-to' positioning (content strength) — or on improving sentence-level clarity, word choice, and rhetorical impact (writing strength)?
